steps.title: Fit the table first
steps.content: This is a linear table question, so use Desmos regression instead of doing all the slope algebra by hand. The table includes $(-6,65)$ and $(6,29)$. Enter those as lists and run $y_1\sim m x_1+b$.

steps.title: Convert to the choice format
steps.content: Desmos gives $m=-3$ and $b=47$, so the table line is $$y=-3x+47$$ Move terms to match the answer choices: $$3x+y=47$$ Multiplying by $3$ gives $$9x+3y=141$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice A is exactly $9x+3y=141$. Choice B has the same left side but the wrong constant. Choices C and D switch the coefficients to $3x+9y$, which does not match the fitted line. Therefore A is correct.
